_comment: Refold-and-validate configs for the binder block. User decision: run THREE oracles (protenix + openfold3 + esmfold2) on the SAME designed sequences and compare both fold quality (DNA-aligned RMSD, ipTM) AND runtime/cost. Each designed protein is folded WITH the target DNA duplex as context, so the fold predicts the protein-DNA complex and yields interface ipTM. Field names follow the respective pecli guides.

protenix._role: AF3-class MSA-based oracle; closest analog to the paper's AlphaFold3 filtering role. Highest expected accuracy, slowest/most expensive.
protenix._msa_note: protenix auto-schedules an MSA step; for de novo designs the MSA will be shallow -- acceptable, this mirrors the paper's setup.

openfold3._role: Second open AF3-class reimplementation; cross-check against protenix on the same designs.

esmfold2._role: MSA-free language-model folder; much faster/cheaper, some accuracy cost. The runtime/cost floor of the comparison.
esmfold2._dsdna_note: esmfold2 does NOT auto-generate the complementary strand -- the complex JSON must list BOTH DNA strands explicitly (second = antiparallel reverse-complement of the first).

complex_json_template._comment: Per-design complex input shared shape (protenix/openfold3/esmfold2 all accept a chains list). Built by scripts/build_fold_inputs.py from each ligandmpnn design sequence + the target DNA. Both DNA strands are always included so the same file works for all three oracles.

comparison_outputs._comment: scripts/filter_binder_block.py records, per design per oracle: DNA-aligned protein Ca-RMSD (design vs oracle-refold), ipTM, protein-DNA H-bond count, plus per-oracle wall-clock and (if available from pecli run metadata) GPU-time/cost. Written to results/binder_block/oracle_comparison.csv.
